ACTIVITIES:THIS AGREEMENT FUNDS PESTICIDE REGULATORY AND ENFORCEMENT ACTIVITIES INCLUDING TRAINING AND CERTIFYING PESTICIDE APPLICATORS, CONDUCTING WORKER PROTECTION ACTIVITIES TO PROTECT FARMWORKERS AND COMMUNITIES, PROTECTING WATER QUALITY BY IDENTIFYING PESTICIDES OF INTEREST, IDENTIFYING LOCATIONS THAT NEED PROTECTION DUE TO THE LOCATION OF ENDANGERED SPECIES AND THEIR HABITAT, PROTECTING PUBLIC HEALTH FROM UNREGISTERED PESTICIDE PRODUCTS AND MISUSE, MONITORING FOR COMPLIANCE IN THE FIELD AND ENFORCING EPA PESTICIDE LABEL RESTRICTIONS WHEN NEEDED, AND CONDUCING OUTREACH AND EDUCATION TO THE REGULATED COMMUNITY AND THE PUBLIC.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:ANTICIPATED DELIVERABLES AND EXPECTED OUTCOMES OF THIS COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT INCLUDE BETTER PROTECTION OF PUBLIC HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT THROUGH 1) INCREASED OUTREACH AND EDUCATION ON PROPER PESTICIDE USE TO THE REGULATED COMMUNITY AND THE PUBLIC TO REDUCE RISK; 2) PROPER TRAINING AND CERTIFICATION OF PESTICIDE APPLICATORS; 3) MONITORING FOR COMPLIANCE BY PERFORMING INSPECTIONS; 4) RESPONDING TO TIPS AND COMPLAINTS; AND 6) TAKING ENFORCEMENT ACTIONS WHEN NEEDED.  THE PROGRAM BENEFICIARIES ARE THE REGULATED COMMUNITY AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC.

DESCRIPTION:THE PURPOSE OF THIS PROPOSAL IS TO STRENGTHEN SUSTAINABLE PEST MANAGEMENT IN THE PACIFIC NORTHWEST (PNW) BY SLOWING THE DEVELOPMENT OF HERBICIDE RESISTANT WEEDS, PRESERVING THE USEFUL LIFE OF CURRENT HERBICIDE PRODUCTS, AND IMPROVING ADOPTION OF EXISTING INTEGRATED PEST MANAGEMENT (IPM) TOOLS. WE WILL ACCOMPLISH THESE GOALS BY TRAINING AGRICULTURAL PROFESSIONALS ON HERBICIDE RESISTANCE PLANNING, MANAGEMENT, AND REMEDIATION, THUS EMPOWERING THESE PROFESSIONALS TO TRANSFER AND AMPLIFY THE SKILLS AND KNOWLEDGE GAINED IN OUR WORKSHOPS TO GROWERS. THIS PROJECT WILL BE BROADLY APPLICABLE TO THE DIVERSITY OF CONVENTIONALLY-PRODUCED FIELD CROP OPERATIONS ON 9.7 MILLION ACRES OF IRRIGATED AND NON-IRRIGATED LAND IN THE PNW, WILL SERVE AS A TEMPLATE FOR PROGRAMS IN OTHER REGIONS OF THE UNITED STATES, AND FOR FUTURE IPM RESISTANCE PROGRAMS TARGETING INSECTS AND DISEASES IN THE PNW.ACTIVITIES:1. YEAR 1: APPLICANT WILL DEVELOP CURRICULA FOR UP TO FOUR ONE-HOUR, WEB-BASED, ON-DEMAND TRAINING MODULES BROADLY APPLICABLE TO THE DIVERSITY OF CONVENTIONALLY-PRODUCED FIELD CROP OPERATIONS ON 9.7 MILLION ACRES OF IRRIGATED AND NON-IRRIGATED LAND IN THE PACIFIC NORTH WEST. ONLINE, INTERACTIVE TRAINING MODULES WILL BE AVAILABLE IN ENGLISH AND SPANISH. APPLICANT WILL DEVELOP TRAINING MATERIALS ON HERBICIDE RESISTANCE MANAGEMENT PLANS, BEST MANAGEMENT PRACTICES, AND REMEDIAL ACTION PLANTS. INFORMATION WILL BE INCLUDED IN ONLINE TRAINING MODULES.
2. YEAR 2: APPLICANT WILL HOST THREE REGION-SPECIFIC, IN-PERSON WORKSHOPS FOR STAKEHOLDERS REPRESENTING OVER 1.58 MILLION ACRES OF AGRICULTURAL LAND IN OREGON'S WILLAMETTE VALLEY, 8 MILLION ACRES OF AGRICULTURAL LAND IN THE COLUMBIA BASIN OF OREGON AND WASHINGTON, AND 387,000 ACRES OF UNDER-REPRESENTED AGRICULTURAL LAND IN THE TREASURE VALLEY OF OREGON AND IDAHO. SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:ACTIVITES:
1. UP TO FOUR ONE-HOUR WEB-BASED TRAINING MODULES ON HERBICIDE RESISTANCE IN PNW SYSTEMS, HOSTED BY OSU'S PACE PLATFORM.
2.THREE REGION-SPECIFIC, IN-PERSON WORKSHOPS ON HERBICIDE RESISTANCE DELIVERED AT OSU'S RESEARCH AND EXPERIMENTS CENTERS TO COVER CROPPING SYSTEMS IN THE WILLAMETTE VALLEY, THE COLUMBIA BASIN OF OREGON AND WASHINGTON, AND THE TREASURE VALLEY OF OREGON AND IDAHO.
3. AN EASILY-ADAPTABLE, TIME-BASED TEACHING PLAN PROVIDING LEARNING OBJECTIVES, LESSON PLANS, ACTIVITIES AND EVALUATION PLANS TO ASSIST WORKSHOP PARTICIPANTS IN TRANSFERRING HERBICIDE
RESISTANCE BMP'S AND PLANNING STRATEGIES.

BENIFICIARIES: PACIFIC NORTHWEST GROWERS, FARM WORKERS AGRICULTURAL PROFESSIONALS.

SHORT-TERM OUTCOMES:
PARTICIPANTS WILL BE ABLE TO 1) IDENTIFY KEY WEED SPECIES THAT HAVE SHOWN RESISTANCE TO HERBICIDES IN THEIR REGIONS, 2) EXPLAIN THE MECHANISMS THAT CONTRIBUTE TO HERBICIDE RESISTANCE BY WEEDS IN AGRICULTURAL SYSTEMS, 3) FIND AND USE AVAILABLE RESOURCES TO SELECT HERBICIDES BASED ON MODES-OF-ACTION FOR REGIONAL RESISTANT WEED SPECIES, 4) RECOGNIZE NON-CHEMICAL WEED MANAGEMENT TACTICS AS PART OF A COMPLETE IPM PROGRAM, 5) UNDERSTAND THE ENVIRONMENTAL AND ECONOMIC RISKS OF HERBICIDE RESISTANCE.
LONG-TERM OUTCOMES:
PARTICIPANTS WILL BE ABLE TO 1) MAKE INFORMED DECISIONS ABOUT WEED MANAGEMENT IN THEIR SYSTEMS, 2) IMPLEMENT WEED MANAGEMENT TECHNIQUES TO PREVENT RESISTANCE FROM OCCURRING, 3) INCORPORATE THESE SKILLS INTO AN IPM PLAN FOR WEEDS IN THEIR SYSTEM 4) TRANSFER KNOWLEDGE OF HERBICIDE RESISTANCE PLANNING STRATEGIES AND BMP'S TO GROWERS AND
FARM WORKERS ACROSS PNW, 5) OFFER EFFECTIVE TRAININGS ON HERBICIDE RESISTANCE ADAPTED TO THEIR REGION OR AUDIENCE, 6) ENCOURAGE SUSTAINABLE PEST MANAGEMENT IN PACIFIC NORTHWEST
AGRICULTURAL SYSTEMS, 7) SLOW THE DEVELOPMENT OF HERBICIDE RESISTANCE IN WEEDS PRESERVE THE USEFUL LIFE OF CURRENT HERBICIDE FORMULATIONS INCREASE IPM PRACTICES FOR WEED MANAGEMENT IN THE PACIFIC NORTHWEST (E.G., DECREASING HERBICIDE DEPENDENCE AND OVERALL HERBICIDE USE), 8) STRENGTHEN ECONOMIC VIAB

DESCRIPTION:UNDER THIS AWARD OREGON STATE UNIVERSITY (OSU) WILL DISTRIBUTE COPIES OF EXPLORE THE BEES OF OREGON ACROSS THE PACIFIC NORTHWEST PORTION OF REGION 10 (EXCLUDING ALASKA) THROUGH THE OREGON STATE UNIVERSITY BEE ADVOCATE PROGRAM. THE ACTIVITY BOOK TARGETS AGES 4 AND ABOVE AND HIGHLIGHTS THE CONNECTION BETWEEN CROPS AND THE BEES OF THE STATE. THE BOOK ALSO DRAWS THE CONNECTION BETWEEN FIRST FOODS AND THE BEES THAT POLLINATE THEM AND WAS DEVELOPED IN PARTNERSHIP WITH THE OREGON STATE UNIVERSITY INDIGENOUS PEOPLE'S WORK GROUP. THE ACTIVITY PAGES CAN BE COLORED, INCLUDE TEARAWAY CARDS FEATURING KEY NATIVE AND MANAGED BEES OF THE STATE AND FEATURE KEY ASPECTS OF BEE BIOLOGY, BOTH IN SPANISH AND ENGLISH.ACTIVITIES:OSU WILL DELIVER 2,005 COPIES OF EXPLORE THE BEES OF OREGON ACROSS THE PACIFIC NORTHWEST PORTION OF REGION 10 (EXCLUDING ALASKA) THROUGH THE OREGON STATE UNIVERSITY BEE ADVOCATE PROGRAM.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:2000 OREGONIANS WILL BE EDUCATED ON THE IMPORTANCE OF BEES TO SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURAL SYSTEMS AND ECOSYSTEMS. STUDENTS ENGAGED BY THE ACTIVITY BOOK WILL: (A) RECOGNIZE THE CONNECTION OF BEES TO CROPS IN THE REGION AND UNDERSTAND WHAT IS BEING DONE TO KEEP BEES FROM PESTICIDE EXPOSURE AND (B) BE LESS AFRAID OF STINGING INSECTS AND WILL BE LESS LIKELY TO USE PESTICIDES WHEN ENCOUNTERING BENEFICIAL INSECTS IN THEIR ADULTHOOD.

DESCRIPTION:THIS ACTION PROVIDES FUNDING IN THE AMOUNT OF $199,999 TO CLEMSON UNIVERSITY, SOUTH CAROLINA FOR THEIR CONTINUING PESTICIDE ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAMS WHILE GIVING IT GREATER FLEXIBILITY TO ADDRESS ITS HIGHEST ENVIRONMENTAL PRIORITIES, IMPROVE ENVIRONMENTAL PERFORMANCE, ACHIEVE ADMINISTRATIVE SAVINGS AND STRENGTHEN THE PARTNERSHIP BETWEEN THE CLEMSON UNIVERSITY AND ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AGENCY (EPA).ACTIVITIES:THIS AGREEMENT FUNDS STATEWIDE PROGRAMS TO: PROVIDE A COMPREHENSIVE PROGRAM OF REGISTRATION, LABELING, TRAINING, CERTIFICATION, INSPECTIONS AND ENFORCEMENT THAT WILL PREVENT UNNECESSARY EXPOSURE TO HUMANS, THE LAND AND WATER BY ENSURING THAT PESTICIDES ARE USED AND DISPOSED OF IN THE MANNER SPECIFIED ON EACH PRODUCT. SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:INSPECTIONS AND ENFORCEMENT WILL DETER NON-COMPLIANCE WITH PRODUCT REQUIREMENTS. FURTHERMORE, WORKERS THEMSELVES WILL BE PROTECTED FROM WORKPLACE EXPOSURE THROUGH IMPLEMENTATION OF TRAINING AND CERTIFICATION PROGRAMS AND THE USE OF INSPECTIONS AND ENFORCEMENT. POLLINATORS AND ENDANGERED SPECIES WILL BE PROTECTED FROM PESTICIDE EXPOSURE THROUGH OUTREACH AND COMPLIANCE ACTIVITIES. ANTICIPATED DELIVERABLES: OVER 20,000 HOURS OF INSPECTIONS; ONGOING CERTIFICATION AND TRAINING OF OVER 20,000 CERTIFIED APPLICATORS; ALL OTHER PROJECT DELIVERABLES, AS DETERMINED BY THE FEDERAL INSECTICIDE, FUNGICIDE, AND RODENTICIDE ACT (FIFRA) COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT GUIDANCE AND THE MUTUALLY AGREED UPON WORKPLAN; MID-YEAR AND END-OF-YEAR REPORTS. INTENDED BENEFICIARIES ARE THE RESIDENTS OF THE STATE OF SOUTH CAROLINA.

DESCRIPTION:THE GOAL OF THIS PROJECT IS TO IMPROVE POLLINATOR PROTECTION AND HEALTH THROUGH TARGETED SURVEYS, TRAININGS, AND EVALUATIONS OF 5,000 NEWLY-TRAINED AND 2,500 PREVIOUSLY-TRAINED PESTICIDE APPLICATORS ACROSS AT LEAST 12 STATES TO ENSURE KNOWLEDGE TRANSFER IS SUCCESSFUL AND IMPACTFUL IN AFFECTING BEHAVIORAL PRACTICES REGARDING PESTICIDE USE AROUND POLLINATING INSECTS THAT ALIGN WITH AIMS OF MANAGED POLLINATOR PROTECTION PLANS. 
ACTIVITIES:#9679; OBJECTIVE 1: ASSESS EDUCATIONAL NEEDS AND TRAINING FORMATS FOR LICENSED U.S. PESTICIDE APPLICATORS.
O OUTPUTS: SURVEY OF ALL SLAS, TRIBES AND PSEP PROGRAMS IN THE US AND ANALYSIS OF TRAINING TO SEE THE EXTENT IT LINES UP WITH RECOMMENDATION FROM EPA ON LABEL AND MITIGATION AND WITH SLA AND TRIBAL MP3.
O OUTCOMES: STATE-SPECIFIC DATA ON PESTICIDE TRAINING SYSTEMS AND REQUIREMENTS THAT WILL AID IN THE DEVELOPMENT OF EFFECTIVE DELIVERY OF OUR TRAINING AS WELL AS OTHERS THAT AIM FOR A NATIONAL OR MULTI-STATE APPROACH
#9679; OBJECTIVE 2: SURVEY PREVIOUSLY TRAINED APPLICATORS AND DEVELOP EVALUATION TOOLS FOR TRAININGS ON LABEL COMPREHENSION AND IMPLEMENTATION OF POLLINATOR PROTECTION PRACTICES.
O OUTPUTS: DATA ON KNOWLEDGE GAINED BY PREVIOUSLY TRAINED APPLICATORS, ALLOWING FOR THE ASSESSMENT OF RETENTION AND BEHAVIORAL CHANGES OVER TIME. EVALUATION TOOL THAT CAN BE INCORPORATED INTO TRAINING DEVELOPED BY MP3 WORKING GROUP ON LABEL COMPREHENSION.
O OUTCOMES: TIME SERIES DATA ON RETENTION OF KNOWLEDGE, ADOPTION OF PRACTICES, AND IMPACT OF RE-TRAINING BASED ON DATA FROM OREGON'S 5+ YEARS OF POLLINATOR LABEL TRAININGS; THIS WILL HELP US UNDERSTAND TOPICS NEEDING MORE EMPHASIS AND TIME PERIODS THAT ARE VALUABLE FOR REINFORCING CONCEPTS. ACCURATE DATA ON POLLINATOR PROTECTION PLANS AND ACTIVITIES CAPTURED AS A RESULT OF EVALUATION TOOLS.
#9679; OBJECTIVE 3: ROLL OUT THE MP3 WORKING GROUP'S PESTICIDE LABEL TRAINING MODULE WITH NEW EVALUATION TOOLS TO AT LEAST 12 STATES.
O OUTPUTS: TRAININGS FOR PSEP STAFF AND EXTENSION ON HOW TO DELIVER THE TRAINING TO APPLICATORS, ANALYSIS OF EVALUATION TOOLS ON WHETHER THE TRAINING WAS EFFECTIVE AT IMPROVING KNOWLEDGE AND INCREASING INTENTION TO ADOPT NEW MITIGATION MEASURES TO PROTECT POLLINATORS.
O OUTCOMES: CONSISTENCY, ACCURACY, AND USABILITY IN THE AVAILABLE TRAINING MATERIALS AND NATIONAL OBJECTIVES THAT ALIGN WITH THE MP3 PESTICIDE LABEL TRAINING MODULE.
SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES:
1. SURVEY OF ALL SLAS, TRIBES AND PSEP PROGRAMS IN THE US AND ANALYSIS OF TRAINING TO SEE THE EXTENT IT LINES UP WITH RECOMMENDATION FROM EPA ON LABEL AND MITIGATION AND WITH SLA AND TRIBAL MP3.
2. DATA ON KNOWLEDGE GAINED BY PREVIOUSLY TRAINED APPLICATORS, ALLOWING FOR THE ASSESSMENT OF RETENTION AND BEHAVIORAL CHANGES OVER TIME. EVALUATION TOOL THAT CAN BE INCORPORATED INTO TRAINING DEVELOPED BY MP3 WORKING GROUP ON LABEL COMPREHENSION.
3. TRAININGS FOR PSEP STAFF AND EXTENSION ON HOW TO DELIVER THE TRAINING TO APPLICATORS, ANALYSIS OF EVALUATION TOOLS ON WHETHER THE TRAINING WAS EFFECTIVE AT IMPROVING KNOWLEDGE AND INCREASING INTENTION TO ADOPT NEW MITIGATION MEASURES TO PROTECT POLLINATORS.

OUTCOMES:
1. STATE-SPECIFIC DATA ON PESTICIDE TRAINING SYSTEMS AND REQUIREMENTS THAT WILL AID IN THE DEVELOPMENT OF EFFECTIVE DELIVERY OF OUR TRAINING AS WELL AS OTHERS THAT AIM FOR A NATIONAL OR MULTI-STATE APPROACH.
2. TIME SERIES DATA ON RETENTION OF KNOWLEDGE, ADOPTION OF PRACTICES, AND IMPACT OF RE-TRAINING BASED ON DATA FROM OREGON'S 5+ YEARS OF POLLINATOR LABEL TRAININGS; THIS WILL HELP US UNDERSTAND TOPICS NEEDING MORE EMPHASIS AND TIME PERIODS THAT ARE VALUABLE FOR REINFORCING CONCEPTS. ACCURATE DATA ON POLLINATOR PROTECTION PLANS AND ACTIVITIES CAPTURED AS A RESULT OF EVALUATION TOOLS.

DESCRIPTION:THIS ACTION APPROVES AN AWARD IN THE AMOUNT OF $55,261 TO GEORGIA DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE TO PROVIDE IMPLEMENT THE 'GEORGIA DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE (GDA) 2022 REGION 4 INTEGRATED PEST MANAGEMENT GRANT' PROJECT.  THIS AWARD WILL ALLOW THE GDA TO REDUCE UNNECESSARY EXPOSURES TO PESTS AND PESTICIDES THROUGH AN EDUCATION INITIATIVE EXPANDING INTEGRATED PEST MANAGEMENT TO ENCOURAGE SMART, SENSIBLE, AND SUSTAINABLE PEST CONTROL IN SCHOOLS AND HOMES. ACTIVITIES:TO REACH STUDENTS, GDA WILL CREATE A CURRICULUM ABOUT STRUCTURAL INTEGRATED PEST MANAGEMENT (IPM) FOR ELEMENTARY SCHOOL STUDENTS. TOPICS COVERED WILL INCLUDE PEST IDENTIFICATION, THE STEPS OF IPM, AND PESTICIDE SAFETY. GDA INSPECTORS WILL USE THIS CURRICULUM IN ELEMENTARY SCHOOL CLASSES ACROSS THE STATE. GDA WILL ALSO USE THE FUNDS TO SPONSOR SCHOOL PEST MANAGEMENT PROFESSIONALS TO ATTEND THE GDA SCHOOL IPM WORKSHOP AT NO COST TO THEM. THIS WILL ENABLE SCHOOL EMPLOYEES, WHO HAD BEEN PREVIOUSLY PROHIBITED BY THE COST OF THE WORKSHOP OR TRAVEL EXPENSES, TO ATTEND. THE CONCEPTS TAUGHT AT THIS WORKSHOP WILL HELP ATTENDEES DEVELOP MORE SUSTAINABLE PEST MANAGEMENT PROGRAMS AND REINFORCE IMPORTANT SAFETY PRINCIPLES TO ENSURE THAT NO STUDENTS ARE EXPOSED TO PESTICIDES IN THEIR SCHOOL ENVIRONMENT.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:ANTICIPATED DELIVERABLES FOR THIS PROJECT INCLUDE A GEORGIA SPECIFIC STRUCTURAL IPM CURRICULUM AIMED AT ELEMENTARY SCHOOL STUDENTS AND PARENTS, AS WELL AS EDUCATION PROVIDED FOR 1440 STUDENTS AND 200 PEST MANAGEMENT PROFESSIONALS. EXPECTED OUTCOMES INCLUDE PROTECTION OF NON-PEST INSECT POPULATIONS, A REDUCTION IN PESTICIDE APPLICATIONS, LOWERED INCIDENCE OF PESTICIDE EXPOSURES, A REDUCTION IN PESTICIDE APPLICATIONS, AND A REDUCTION IN SCHOOL PEST MANAGEMENT COSTS. INTENDED BENEFICIARIES OF THE PROJECT INCLUDE THE STUDENTS AND PEST MANAGEMENT PROFESSIONALS OF ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE TARGET SCHOOLS, AS WELL AS TEACHERS AND THE PARENTS OF THOSE STUDENTS.

Recipient: UNIVERSITY OF VERMONT & STATE AGRICULTURAL COLLEGE
Description:
DESCRIPTION:THE GOAL OF THIS PROJECT IS TO REDUCE NEONICOTINOID SEED TREATMENTS (NSTS) APPLIED TO ROW CROPS IN VERMONT. THE OBJECTIVE IS TO PROVIDE FARMERS EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE WITH SCOUTING AND ASSESSING ACTION THRESHOLDS FOR EARLY SEASON PESTS IN ROW CROPS. THROUGH THIS NEWLY GAINED KNOWLEDGE FARMERS WILL BE ABLE TO MAKE MORE INFORMED DECISIONS AS TO WHETHER A NST IS NEEDED TO PREVENT ANY CROP LOSSES. THE OUTPUTS OF THE PROJECT INCLUDE SIX ON-FARM DEMONSTRATIONS, 30 ONE-ON-ONE CONSULTATIONS, SIX ON-FARM FIELDS DAYS, ONE WEBINAR SERIES, TWO CROP SPECIFIC SCOUTING FACTSHEETS, AND EIGHT PRESENTATIONS TO GROWER AND STAKEHOLDER AUDIENCES. THE APPLICANT EXPECTS TO EDUCATE OVER 1000 NORTHEASTERN GROWERS ON IPM PRACTICES, PROVIDE 250 FARMERS WITH HANDS-ON TRAINING, AND REDUCE NSTS ON AT LEAST 1500 ACRES.ACTIVITIES:A VARIETY OF ACTIVITIES WILL OCCUR TO EDUCATE FARMERS ON HOW TO SCOUT FOR SOIL PESTS, EVALUATE THRESHOLDS, AND DETERMINE PEST MANAGEMENT ACTIONS IN CORN AND SOYBEAN FIELDS IN VERMONT. ULTIMATELY, HELPING FARMERS IMPLEMENT INTEGRATED PEST MANAGEMENT (IPM) PRACTICES TO AVOID SUPERFLUOUS NSTS IF THEY ARE NOT NECESSARY.

1. SIX PARTNER FARMS WILL SERVE AS DEMONSTRATION SITES WHERE SCOUTING, THRESHOLDS, AND ACTIONS ARE TAKEN TO DETERMINE IF PESTICIDES ARE WARRANTED. TEN ACRES OF CORN OR SOYBEANS WILL BE USED FOR THE DEMONSTRATION ON EACH FARM. HALF THE FIELD WILL USE THE FARMER'S CURRENT PRACTICES AND HALF OF THE  FIELD WILL USE IPM PRACTICES. THESE FIELDS WILL SERVE AS EDUCATIONAL SITES WHERE FARMERS AND OTHER AGRICULTURAL INDUSTRY STAKEHOLDERS CAN LEARN ABOUT IPM TACTICS FOR SOIL PESTS THAT IMPACT CORN AND SOYBEAN.
2. THIRTY PARTNER FARMS WILL BE ACTIVE PARTICIPANTS IN THE TWO-YEAR PROJECT TO GAIN DIRECT EXPERIENCE AND ONE-ON-ONE TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE WITH SCOUTING, ACTION THRESHOLDS, AND MANAGEMENT DECISIONS. THESE FARMS WILL WORK WITH PROJECT TEAM TO SELECT AT LEAST ONE FIELD TO SCOUT, RECEIVE SCOUTING
TOOLS, AND ASSISTANCE WITH SCOUTING AND ACTION THRESHOLDS. 
3. WEBINAR SERIES - A WINTER PEST MANAGEMENT WEBINAR SERIES WILL BE HELD IN JAN/FEB OF 2022. THE WEBINAR SERIES WILL HIGHLIGHT PESTS OF CORN AND SOYBEANS, SCOUTING AND MONITORING OF THESE PESTS, ACTION THRESHOLDS, AND PEST MANAGEMENT TOOLS. ENVIRONMENTAL CONCERNS RELATED TO PESTICIDES AND OVERUSE WILL ALSO BE COVERED.
4. ON-FARM FIELD DAYS - THREE FIELD DAYS PER YEAR HELD AT DEMONSTRATION FARMS WILL PROVIDE PARTICIPANTS WITH HANDS-ON TRAINING FOR SCOUTING, IDENTIFICATION, AND ACTION THRESHOLDS.
PRESENTATION AT LOCAL AND REGIONAL WINTER CONFERENCES - NO-TILL COVER CROP CONFERENCE, GRAIN CONFERENCE, DAIRY MEETINGS, AND CERTIFIED CROP ADVISOR CONFERENCES WILL SERVE AS A PLATFORM TO DELIVER PROJECT INFORMATION TO OUR TARGET AUDIENCE. IN 2022, AN IPM INTENSIVE WILL BE HELD AT THE ANNUAL NO-TILL COVER CROP CONFERENCE TO TRAIN STAKEHOLDERS AND IN 2023 A FARMER-TO-FARMER SESSION TO HIGHLIGHT IPM OUTCOMES. SCOUTING FACTSHEETS FOR CORN AND SOYBEAN - FACTSHEETS HIGHLIGHTING PROTOCOLS, ACTION THRESHOLDS, AND MANAGEMENT OPTIONS WILL BE DEVELOPED AND POSTED ON THE WWW.UVM.EDU/NWCROPS WEBSITE.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES:
1. CONDUCT 6 ON-FARM DEMONSTRATION SITES THAT ENGAGE KEY FARMERS IN ADOPTING IPM STRATEGIES FOR MANAGING SOIL PESTS IN CORN AND SOYBEANS IN 2022 AND 2023.
2. CONDUCT 6 ON-FARM FIELD DAYS AT THE DEMONSTRATION SITES THAT PROVIDE HANDS-ON IPM TRAINING TO FARMERS AND
AGRICULTURAL INDUSTRY STAKEHOLDERS ON IPM STRATEGIES FOR MANAGEMENT OF SOIL PESTS IN CORN AND SOYBEANS IN 2022 AND 2023.
3. COORDINATE WITH 30 PARTNER FARMS (2000 ACRES) WILL RECEIVE ONE-ON-ONE TRAINING AND TOOLS TO IMPLEMENT SCOUTING AND ACTION THRESHOLDS IN 2021 AND 2022.
4. DEVELOP AND PRESENT A 4-PART WEBINAR SERIES FOCUSED ON SOIL PESTS OF CORN AND SOYBEANS, SCOUTING, THRESHOLDS, AND IPM STRATEGIES TO REDUCE THE PROPHYLACTIC USE OF NEONICOTINOIDS IN THE WINTER OF 2022.

Recipient: THE NATIONAL ASSOCIATION OF STATE DEPARTMENTS OF AGRICULTURE RESEARCH FOUNDATION
Description:
DESCRIPTION:THE PURPOSE OF THIS PROJECT, THE PIRT PROGRAM, IS TO PROVIDE PESTICIDE EDUCATIONAL TRAINING TO INSPECTORS AND STAFF OF STATES, TRIBES, AND U.S. TERRITORIES WORKING UNDER FIFRA COOPERATIVE AGREEMENTS, TO DEVELOP OR ENHANCE THEIR ABILITY TO FORMULATE AND IMPLEMENT PESTICIDE REGULATORY PROGRAMS, AND/OR UNDERTAKE NEW INITIATIVES. THIS PROJECT WILL DEVELOP AT LEAST THREE TO FOUR NATIONAL PIRTS ANNUALLY, IN ADDITION TO A TRIBAL PIRT BIANNUALLY, COVERING TOPICS RELEVANT TO CONDUCTING AGRICULTURE PESTICIDE INSPECTIONS. THESE TRAINING OPPORTUNITIES WILL BE HELD IN DIFFERENT LOCATIONS ACROSS THE COUNTRY, AND WILL IMPROVE STAFF CAPACITY TO ADDRESS NONCOMPLIANCE ISSUES AND SAFETY GAPS, LEADING TO MORE EFFECTIVE PROTECTION OF HUMAN HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT. WITHIN AND BEYOND THE IN-PERSON TRAININGS, NASDA RESEARCH FOUNDATION HAS OUTLINED PLANS TO MAKE SPACE FOR NETWORKING AMONG ATTENDEES WHEREVER THEY ARE TO BUILD COMMUNITY AND FOSTER COOPERATION AMONG STATES, TRIBES, U.S. TERRITORIES, AND U.S. GOVERNMENT AGENCIES - AN IMPORTANT STEP TOWARD INCREASING EFFICIENCIES AND STRETCHING LIMITED REGULATORY DOLLARS. IN ADDITION, THIS PROJECT WILL ALSO DO CONTINUAL EVALUATION. PARTICIPANTS WILL BE PRIMED TO PROVIDE CONSTRUCTIVE FEEDBACK VIA PAPER OR ELECTRONIC SURVEYS, EVEN MONTHS AFTER THEIR INITIAL CONTACT WITH THE NASDA RESEARCH FOUNDATION. THIS TYPE OF PERSONAL RESPONSE, COMBINED WITH ANALYSIS OF WEBSITE ACCESS STATISTICS, AMONG OTHER MEASURES WILL HELP NASDA RESEARCH FOUNDATION EVALUATE PROGRESS, AND CONTINUALLY IMPROVE THE PIRT PROGRAM.ACTIVITIES:ACTIVITIES THAT NASDA RESEARCH FOUNDATION WILL UNDERTAKE UNDER THIS PROJECT ARE:
1. ASSESS THE TRAINING NEEDS OF THE PIRT CLIENTELE BY SUMMARIZING NEEDS EXPRESSED BY PARTICIPANTS ON COURSE EVALUATION FORMS AND OTHER METHODS. ASSIST IN PREPARING ANY OTHER SUMMARY INFORMATION AS REQUESTED BY THE PIRT COORDINATOR.
2. UNDERTAKE MANAGEMENT OF THE PIRT STEERING COMMITTEE AND MEMBERSHIP SELECTION PROCESS FROM PIRT COORDINATOR. 
           A. COORDINATE STEERING COMMITTEE MEETINGS TO CREATE COURSE AGENDAS OR TRAINING APPROACHES.
3. CONDUCT BETWEEN THREE TO FOUR NATIONAL PIRTS A YEAR PLUS ONE TRIBAL PIRT EVERY OTHER YEAR, LASTING THREE TO FOUR DAYS FOR 25-30 PARTICIPANTS PER COURSE. THE LOCATIONS OF THE TRAINING COURSES SHOULD VARY WITHIN THE UNITED STATES TO TAKE ADVANTAGE OF DIFFERENT ENVIRONMENTS AND TYPES OF PESTICIDE USAGE ACROSS THE COUNTRY. FOR TRIBAL PIRTS, THE PREFERENCE IS TO WORK WITH A TRIBE/S WHO HAVE A FIFRA COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT, IF POSSIBLE. 
          A. RETAIN APPROPRIATE TRAINING CONSULTANTS AND INSTRUCTORS BASED ON COURSE NEEDS, TRAINER AVAILABILITY, AND COURSE BUDGET. THE COURSE CONTENT WILL GENERALLY FOCUS ON PESTICIDE

              COMPLIANCE AND ENFORCEMENT ISSUES. THE COURSE CONTENT MAY ALSO INCLUDE ADDITIONAL TOPICS NEEDED TO SUPPORT EFFECTIVE STATE AND TRIBAL PESTICIDE PROGRAMS SUCH AS RISK 
              COMMUNICATION TRAINING, ISSUES RELATED TO HOMELAND SECURITY, LABORATORY ISSUES, NEW REGULATORY PROGRAMS, AND EMERGING ISSUES.

           C. COLLECT AND COLLATE COURSE MATERIALS FROM INSTRUCTORS AND PRESENTERS IN A TIMELY MANNER AND ARRANGE FOR THEIR DISTRIBUTION PRIOR TO EACH COURSE.
           D. SECURE CLASSROOM SPACE FOR ALL COURSES.
